rows.title: Quantifier-free fixed-width bit-vectors
rows.scope: Bool plus fixed-width BitVec terms, lowered through AIG/CNF/SAT with original-term model replay.

partial: Lean reconstruction for the full arithmetic/shift operator set

parser.notes: Scalar bit-vector SMT-LIB is accepted and exercised by committed QF_BV benchmark rows.

ir.notes: Typed Bool/BV terms lower to AIG and CNF with retained lift maps for model replay.

solver_routes.name: bit-blast-to-SAT
solver_routes.notes: The default pure-Rust finite-domain route bit-blasts supported operators and checks SAT models against original terms.

model_replay.notes: Every reported SAT model is replayed over the original terms rather than trusted from the backend.

proof_routes.name: DRAT/LRAT-style SAT certificate after bit-blast
proof_routes.notes: UNSAT certificates are independently checked; Lean-kernel reconstruction is complete only for named sub-fragments.

benchmarks.name: qf-bv-curated-bvred
benchmarks.notes: Small audited BV reduction row; hard public p4dfa remains the broader performance frontier.

dominance.notes: Dominant on the committed curated BV reduction audit row, not a blanket claim for every BV shape.

open_gaps: Hard QF_BV performance on adversarial public slices
Complete Lean reconstruction for every BV arithmetic and shift route

rows.title: Quantifier-free arrays over bit-vectors
rows.scope: Bounded SMT arrays with BitVec index and element sorts, using read-over-write simplification, Ackermann-style reduction, and replay.

supported_public: Bool/BV operators on indices and elements

parser.notes: The supported public slice is bounded and intentionally narrower than all SMT-LIB array combinations.

ir.notes: Array values are represented for supported component sorts and projected back for replay.

solver_routes.name: array reduction to Bool/BV plus SAT
solver_routes.notes: Read-over-write and finite array reductions feed the finite-domain solver when the fragment is supported.

model_replay.notes: SAT array models are checked against original array assertions after projection.

proof_routes.name: array reduction evidence plus SAT certificate
proof_routes.notes: Several ROW and finite-array unsat shapes are certified, but broad array elimination is still tracked as partial trust.

benchmarks.name: qf-abv-cvc5-bitwuzla-regress-clean
benchmarks.notes: Strong measured row with no oracle disagreement.

dominance.notes: Dominant on the audited decided subset; remaining work is classification and wider proof coverage.

open_gaps: Classify array unsats by ROW/congruence/general elimination
Grow extensionality and non-BV-array proof coverage

rows.title: Quantifier-free uninterpreted functions
rows.scope: EUF over declared functions and carrier sorts, with congruence closure, model construction, and checked unsat explanations.

partial: mixed fragments with hard arithmetic frontiers

parser.notes: Declared sorts and functions are accepted in the supported EUF command surface.

ir.notes: Function applications and uninterpreted carrier values are modeled without exposing backend lifetimes.

solver_routes.name: congruence closure / e-graph
solver_routes.notes: Backtrackable congruence closure decides the EUF slice and provides explanations for conflicts.

model_replay.notes: SAT function interpretations replay against original assertions.

proof_routes.name: congruence explanation / Alethe / Lean
proof_routes.notes: Pure EUF unsat explanations are re-derived and reconstructed for covered shapes.

benchmarks.name: qf-uf-cvc5-regress-clean-bounded-uninterp-sorts
benchmarks.notes: Mid measured row after first-class carrier-sort handling.

dominance.notes: The decided audited subset is dominant; decide-rate remains the practical gap.

open_gaps: Improve decide-rate on broader declared-sort corpora
Keep Lean audit current as EUF plus mixed-fragment routes expand

rows.title: Uninterpreted functions over bit-vectors
rows.scope: EUF terms whose arguments or results are bit-vectors, combined with finite-domain BV solving.

partial: proof classification for BV arithmetic-heavy unsats

parser.notes: The parser accepts mixed UF and BV terms in the measured benchmark slices.

ir.notes: Function values can be projected with full Axeyum Value keys, including BV values.

solver_routes.name: EUF/Ackermann plus BV solving
solver_routes.notes: UF consistency is combined with the BV backend and replayed before returning SAT.

model_replay.notes: Projected function interpretations are replayed over the original mixed UF/BV assertions.

proof_routes.name: EUF congruence plus BV certificate
proof_routes.checker: congruence checker plus finite-domain evidence checker
proof_routes.notes: Audited rows are certified; wider coverage depends on whether the BV side stays in Lean-covered sub-fragments.

benchmarks.name: qf-ufbv-cvc5-regress-clean
benchmarks.notes: Small measured row with full decided coverage.

dominance.notes: Dominant on the audited cvc5 row and the small bitwuzla row.

open_gaps: Broaden beyond tiny measured rows
Separate UF-only proof obligations from BV arithmetic proof gaps

rows.title: Quantifier-free linear real arithmetic
rows.scope: Linear constraints over exact rationals/reals, decided by exact simplex with replay and Farkas-style unsat evidence.

parser.notes: Linear real arithmetic terms are accepted and routed to the arithmetic solver.

ir.notes: Exact rational arithmetic is modeled in the solver layer while terms remain in the typed IR.

solver_routes.name: exact-rational simplex / DPLL(LRA)
solver_routes.notes: QF_LRA is complete for the supported linear fragment; Boolean structure is handled by the arithmetic dispatcher.

model_replay.notes: SAT arithmetic models are replayed before being returned.

proof_routes.name: Farkas / Alethe la_generic / Lean
proof_routes.notes: Unsat linear-real certificates are independently checked and have Lean/Alethe routes for covered shapes.

benchmarks.name: qf-lra-cvc5-regress-clean
benchmarks.notes: Strong measured row with no disagreement.

dominance.notes: Dominant on the audited decided row; remaining work is wider corpus depth and proof-route reporting.

open_gaps: Run and expose per-instance Lean reconstruction coverage over larger LRA slices
Improve performance on harder neutral corpora

rows.title: Quantifier-free linear integer arithmetic
rows.scope: Linear integer constraints with branch-and-bound/cuts, replayed models, and certified sub-fragment refutations.

partial: hard nonlinear terms routed to NIA
declined: underspecified division/modulo by non-syntactic nonzero divisors where required for sound SMT-LIB replay

parser.notes: Integer arithmetic command and term forms are accepted in the supported fragment.

ir.notes: Integer terms are represented in the IR and solved by arithmetic routes that fail closed to unknown when needed.

solver_routes.name: branch-and-bound simplex plus integer cuts
solver_routes.notes: The general solver is useful but proof coverage is stronger on named integer infeasibility sub-fragments.

model_replay.notes: SAT integer assignments replay against original assertions.

proof_routes.name: Diophantine and interval integer certificates
proof_routes.notes: Equality-system and single-variable interval contradictions are checked; general cut reconstruction remains future work.

benchmarks.name: qf-lia-cvc5-regress-clean
benchmarks.notes: Strong measured row, with one remaining missed decision versus the row size.

dominance.notes: Dominant on the audited decided row; the atlas keeps general LIA proof coverage separate from sub-fragment certificates.

open_gaps: General integer cut proof reconstruction
Harder neutral corpora beyond the small cvc5 slice

rows.title: Quantifier-free datatypes
rows.scope: Datatype constructor/tester/selector reasoning, including axiom-free Lean reconstruction for covered structural contradictions.

parser.notes: Datatype commands and match desugaring exist for the supported command surface.

ir.notes: Datatype constructors, selectors, testers, and structural contradictions are represented in the IR/solver layer.

solver_routes.name: datatype structural reasoning
solver_routes.notes: Covered QF_DT rows decide through structural datatype reasoning.

model_replay.notes: Datatype SAT and structural UNSAT paths retain original-term checking.

proof_routes.name: axiom-free Lean datatype chain
proof_routes.checker: in-tree Lean kernel plus optional real Lean cross-check
proof_routes.notes: Constructor distinctness, injectivity, testers, and single-level acyclicity have checked Lean routes.

benchmarks.name: qf-dt-cvc5-regress-clean
benchmarks.notes: Tiny measured row with complete decided coverage.

dominance.notes: Dominant on the audited QF_DT row, with broader datatype coverage still intentionally scoped.

open_gaps: Broaden measured datatype corpus
Complete multi-step cycles and parametric datatype edges

rows.title: Quantifier-free floating point
rows.scope: SMT floating-point operators lowered to BitVec circuits, with differential validation and replay.

partial: proofs for FP-to-BV lowering remain partial-trust

parser.notes: FP sorts and operators are accepted for supported formats and conversions.

ir.notes: FP is lowered through BitVec circuits rather than exposed as a full independent IR sort.

solver_routes.name: FP-to-BV circuit lowering
solver_routes.notes: Supported FP operations lower to circuits and then reuse finite-domain solving.

model_replay.notes: Models are checked through the original FP semantics supported by the lowering.

proof_routes.name: bit-vector certificate modulo FP lowering trust
proof_routes.checker: BV evidence checker plus differential FP lowering tests
proof_routes.notes: The audited row is dominant, but FP-to-BV certificate coverage is still tracked separately from pure BV proof coverage.

benchmarks.name: qf-fp-bitwuzla-regress-clean
benchmarks.notes: Strong measured row with full decided coverage.

dominance.notes: Dominant on the audited row, while FP-to-BV trust holes remain explicit.

open_gaps: Grow FP-to-BV lowering certificates
Keep nonstandard format support fail-closed

rows.title: Quantifier-free nonlinear real arithmetic
rows.scope: Nonlinear real arithmetic with complete routes for selected algebraic/SOS/CAD slices and sound-incomplete fallback elsewhere.

parser.notes: NRA terms are accepted and dispatched to exact or sound-incomplete nonlinear routes.

ir.notes: Polynomial real terms and real-algebraic witnesses are represented in the solver layer.

solver_routes.name: single-variable algebraic, degree-2 SOS, CAD, and McCormick fallback
solver_routes.notes: Several important slices decide exactly; the hard high-degree tail returns unknown rather than unsound answers.

model_replay.notes: SAT witnesses, including algebraic values, are checked by exact sign/evaluation routines before return.

proof_routes.name: degree-2 SOS / PSD certificate
proof_routes.notes: Degree-2 SOS has checked evidence and selected Lean coverage; general CAD/NRA unsat certificates remain open.

benchmarks.name: qf-nra-synthetic-graduated
benchmarks.notes: Synthetic graduated row exercises covered nonlinear routes.

benchmarks.name: qf-nra-cvc5-regress-clean
benchmarks.notes: Harder cvc5 row remains weak and names the high-degree nonlinear frontier.

dominance.notes: Dominant on the synthetic audited row only; the cvc5 high-degree row is not a dominance claim.

open_gaps: High-degree cvc5-regress nonlinear decision rate
General CAD/NRA unsat proof reconstruction

rows.title: Quantifier-free nonlinear integer arithmetic
rows.scope: Nonlinear integer arithmetic with linear abstraction, guarded bounded bit-blast, exact sub-fragments, replay, and unknown-safe declines.

parser.notes: NIA syntax is accepted and routed through sound-incomplete nonlinear integer procedures.

ir.notes: Nonlinear integer terms are represented exactly; bounded finite routes are guarded to preserve original semantics.

solver_routes.name: linear abstraction plus guarded bounded bit-blast and exact sub-fragments
solver_routes.notes: Small SAT witnesses and named exact sub-fragments decide; genuine nonlinear integer unsat may return unknown.

model_replay.notes: SAT witnesses are checked over exact integer semantics, not trusted from bounded encodings alone.

proof_routes.name: Diophantine/interval sub-fragment certificates
proof_routes.checker: integer certificate checker; fail-closed proof export on guarded blasts
proof_routes.notes: Some integer unsat sub-fragments are certified; bounded nonlinear proof export declines when guards make the certificate non-transferable.

benchmarks.name: qf-nia-synthetic-graduated
benchmarks.notes: Synthetic graduated row exercises the covered NIA routes.

dominance.notes: Dominant on the synthetic audited row only; broader NIA remains a frontier.

open_gaps: General nonlinear-integer unsat proof
Improve broader cvc5-regress decide-rate without weakening guarded replay
